source_pr.title: planner: stabilize tests in `binary_plan_test.go`
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?


Issue Number: close #40004  close #41297

Problem Summary:

Try to fix the above two unstable test cases.

### What is changed and how it works?

1. `TestTooLongBinaryPlan`: I'm unable to reproduce this fail. But I suspect this is because this case is affected by other cases. So I disable and then enable stmt summary in this case to trigger clear the stmt summary in memory, hoping this can fix the problem.
2. `TestLongBinaryPlan`: I added a rather strict check when I wrote this test case. It looks like this is too strict and even small changes might break this case. So I removed some non-necessary checks.
3. I also reset the global variables in the test cases of this file to reduce the possibility of test cases affecting each other.
4. As @hawkingrei advised, update timeout of planner/core to "short".

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Tests in planner/core/binary_plan_test.go are unstable due to cross-test interference from global sysvars (stmt summary/binary plan/slow log threshold) and overly strict assertions on binary plan characteristics (e.g. encoded plan size thresholds).
failure_signature: binary_plan_test: assertions fail intermittently due to global sysvar state leakage (tidb_enable_stmt_summary/tidb_generate_binary_plan/tidb_slow_log_threshold) and brittle size-based checks
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness was caused by (1) shared global state: several tests mutate global sysvars (tidb_generate_binary_plan, tidb_enable_stmt_summary, tidb_slow_log_threshold) without reliably restoring them, so other tests can affect outcomes; and (2) brittle assertions tied to exact/borderline binary plan properties (e.g. size thresholds) that can change with small planner/encoding differences. The fix restores the mutated global sysvars via `defer`, explicitly clears stmt summary state in TestTooLongBinaryPlan, and relaxes overly strict checks / adjusts the test input size (partition count) to reduce sensitivity.
fix_pattern: Restore global sysvars after tests and relax brittle binary-plan assertions (avoid borderline size thresholds / adjust input size) to reduce cross-test interference and plan variability.

Triage summary: planner: stabilize tests in `binary_plan_test.go`
Changed files (2): planner/core/BUILD.bazel, planner/core/binary_plan_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
